skill.name: clickup-automation
skill.description: Automate ClickUp project management including tasks, spaces, folders, lists, comments, and team operations via Rube MCP (Composio). Always search tools first for current schemas.
skill.summary: Automate ClickUp workflows including task creation, hierarchy navigation, comments, and team management through Composio's ClickUp toolkit.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 188 potential 'external command' patterns in SKILL.md, but all are false positives. The patterns are Markdown code formatting (backticks) used for ClickUp API tool names like CLICKUP_GET_AUTHORIZED_TEAMS_WORKSPACES and RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS. These are documentation references, not executable code. The 'network' finding at line 22 is also a false positive - it is a URL reference in documentation. No actual code execution, network calls, or security risks exist in this skill file.

content.value_statement: Manage ClickUp projects through natural language. Create tasks, navigate workspaces, add comments, and automate team workflows without manual data entry.
actual_capabilities: Create and update tasks in ClickUp lists with status, priority, assignees, and due dates
Navigate ClickUp workspace hierarchy (workspaces, spaces, folders, lists)
Add and manage comments on tasks with assignee notifications
Filter and query tasks by status, assignee, tags, dates, and custom fields
View workspace members, seat utilization, and team information
Create subtasks and manage task relationships within lists
limitations: Requires Rube MCP server connection and active ClickUp OAuth authorization
ClickUp rate limits may restrict bulk operations (recommended: set notify_all=false for batch tasks)
Task status values are case-sensitive and must exactly match list-specific configured statuses
Some features like CLICKUP_GET_USER are only available on ClickUp Enterprise plans

use_cases.title: Project manager creates weekly sprint tasks
use_cases.description: A project manager needs to create 20 tasks for the upcoming sprint, assign them to different team members, set priorities, and add due dates. They describe the requirements in natural language, and the skill creates all tasks in the correct ClickUp list with proper formatting.

use_cases.title: Developer updates task status during standup
use_cases.description: A developer wants to quickly move their assigned tasks from 'In Progress' to 'Review' and add comments describing what was completed. The skill locates their tasks and updates status and comments in bulk.

use_cases.title: Team lead reviews workspace capacity
use_cases.description: A team lead needs to check current seat utilization, view active members across multiple workspaces, and identify which lists have the most tasks assigned. The skill provides a summary of workspace metrics and task distribution.

prompt_templates.title: Create a simple task
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a task in ClickUp called 'Review documentation' with status 'To Do', priority 'High', and assign it to me
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick task creation for personal task tracking

prompt_templates.title: Create multiple related tasks
prompt_templates.prompt: Create 5 tasks for the Q1 launch in the Marketing list: 'Design landing page' (urgent), 'Write copy' (high), 'Set up analytics' (normal), 'Test checkout flow' (high), 'Prepare social posts' (normal). Assign all to Sarah and set due dates for next Friday.
prompt_templates.scenario: Batch task creation with different priorities and shared due dates

prompt_templates.title: Query and filter tasks
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me all tasks in the 'Backend API' list that are assigned to John, have status 'In Progress', and are due within the next 7 days. Include subtasks.
prompt_templates.scenario: Finding specific tasks based on multiple filter criteria

prompt_templates.title: Navigate workspace and add comments
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the task 'Fix login bug' in the Engineering workspace, Backend folder, API list. Add a comment: 'Deployed hotfix to production, please verify.' and assign the comment to QA lead.
prompt_templates.scenario: Locating tasks by name and adding contextual comments

output_examples.input: Create a task 'Deploy to staging' with high priority in the DevOps list, assign to Mike, due tomorrow at 5pm
output_examples.output:
Task created successfully:
- Name: Deploy to staging
- List: DevOps
- Status: To Do
- Priority: High (2)
- Assignee: Mike
- Due Date: 2026-02-26 17:00:00
- Task ID: 9hzabc123

View task: https://clickup.com/t/9hzabc123

output_examples.input: List all tasks in 'Sprint 24' with status 'In Progress' or 'Code Review'
output_examples.output:
Found 8 tasks:

1. Implement user authentication (In Progress) - Assigned to: Anna
2. Add password reset flow (Code Review) - Assigned to: Carlos
3. Build API rate limiter (In Progress) - Assigned to: David
4. Design database schema (Code Review) - Assigned to: Elena

All tasks are due within 3 days. 2 subtasks included.

output_examples.input: Add comment 'Tested on Chrome, Firefox, Safari - all passing' to task 'Browser compatibility check'
output_examples.output:
Comment added to task 'Browser compatibility check' (9hzxyz789):

"Tested on Chrome, Firefox, Safari - all passing"

Assigned to: QA Lead
Notifications sent to task watchers.

best_practices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first before using any ClickUp tools to get the current API schemas
Use CLICKUP_GET_LIST to check available status names before creating tasks - status values are case-sensitive and list-specific
For bulk task creation, set notify_all=false to avoid spamming team members with notifications
When working with dates, remember ClickUp uses Unix timestamps in milliseconds (multiply seconds by 1000)
Navigate the workspace hierarchy in order: Workspace -> Space -> Folder -> List to get the correct IDs
anti_patterns: Don't assume status names like 'To Do' exist - always fetch available statuses from the specific list first
Don't create more than 20-30 tasks in rapid succession without checking rate limits to avoid 429 errors
Don't use 'team_id' to refer to user groups - in ClickUp API, 'team_id' means Workspace ID
Don't pass empty strings to clear task descriptions - use a single space ' ' instead
Don't try to create subtasks with another subtask as the parent - subtask parents must be top-level tasks

faq.question: What do I need to set up before using this skill?
faq.answer: You need the Rube MCP server connected (add https://rube.app/mcp to your MCP configuration) and an active ClickUp connection. Run RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS with toolkit 'clickup' and complete OAuth if needed.

faq.question: Why does my task creation fail with 'status not found' error?
faq.answer: Status values are case-sensitive and must exactly match a status configured in your specific ClickUp list. Use CLICKUP_GET_LIST to see the exact status names available in that list.

faq.question: How do I find the correct list_id for creating tasks?
faq.answer: Navigate the hierarchy: Start with CLICKUP_GET_AUTHORIZED_TEAMS_WORKSPACES to get your workspace ID, then CLICKUP_GET_SPACES, then CLICKUP_GET_FOLDERS (or CLICKUP_GET_FOLDERLESS_LISTS for lists not in folders), and finally CLICKUP_GET_LIST to validate the list.

faq.question: Can I create tasks in bulk without hitting rate limits?
faq.answer: Yes, but set notify_all=false for bulk operations and add delays between requests. ClickUp enforces rate limits, so creating more than 20-30 tasks quickly may trigger 429 responses.

faq.question: What's the difference between team_id and user groups?
faq.answer: In ClickUp's API, 'team_id' refers to the Workspace ID, not a user group. To manage user groups, use CLICKUP_GET_TEAMS which returns team groups within a workspace.

faq.question: Why are my due dates showing up as wrong times?
faq.answer: ClickUp expects Unix timestamps in milliseconds. If you provide seconds, multiply by 1000. For example, 5pm on February 26, 2026 would be approximately 1740592400000 (milliseconds).
